findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised opening exchange around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:37 forces Doug into the same immediate failure without using "Tell me something funny" or any near-verbatim calibration line from the style bible; FAIL if the new prompt can still be traced back to an example line.
findings.problem: Simone's opening prompt reproduces a calibration-only example line from the style bible verbatim: "Tell me something funny." Example lines are forbidden for direct reuse, so the chapter imports guide text instead of generating fresh character speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the opening pressure beat in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:39, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:55 with new Simone wording that still demands on-the-spot humor and triggers Doug's stall, but does not echo any example line from outline/style_bible.json.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised middle contains at least one fully dramatized post-Tuesday session in which the reader can quote Doug's failed material, observe Simone's live response, and track a concrete change in method; FAIL if those days are still mainly resumable as "they met several times and she noticed a pattern."
findings.problem: After a strong live opening, the chapter retreats into a Tuesday-through-Friday montage that reports Doug's failed attempts instead of making the reader sit through one. That is sanitization by summary-replacing-scene: the humiliating, comedy-defining development work gets compressed into tidy retrospective explanation, which softens the discomfort the premise requires.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the middle section across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:141 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:217 so at least one post-Tuesday session plays as a full scene. Put one of Doug's bad performative attempts on the page, let it die audibly, and let Simone redirect him in real time before the Friday diagnosis lands.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after the revised turn around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:241, each subsequent beat performs distinct dramatic work and the ending around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:311 conveys Doug's shift through image, action, or partial realization rather than a full prose unpacking of the claim-versus-roses thesis; FAIL if the section still reads like the same insight being proven multiple times.
findings.problem: The chapter's central revelation lands when Simone says the toast worked because Doug was not trying and his ordinary specifics are the material. From there, the scene keeps paraphrasing the same diagnosis in dialogue and narration instead of buying a new turn. The result is deflated comedy and non-award-level compression: the chapter explains its own insight rather than trusting the scene to carry it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the post-diagnosis run from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:241 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:313 so each remaining beat changes pressure. Keep one Doug objection, one sharper Simone counter, and one ending image or action that carries the roses distinction, but cut or rewrite passages that restate "your life is the material" without adding new cost, decision, or information.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Simone's revised Friday exchange is built from shorter diagnostic turns, leaves at least one implication unstated for Doug to infer, and no longer hinges on a polished analogy that could stand alone as writing advice; FAIL if she still delivers a clean mentor monologue that fully interprets Doug for the reader.
findings.problem: Simone slides from dry, diagnostic comedy-writer into polished mentor. Her Friday explanations arrive as balanced mini-speeches and clean analogies, especially the documentary-versus-feature-film line, which reads like theme delivery rather than spoken thought under scene pressure. That blurs her idiolect and violates the style bible's ban on mentor-figure wisdom speeches.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Simone's Friday dialogue in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:237 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:277 into shorter, more interruptible, more character-true turns. Let her identify what works, but make her sound like a working comic diagnostician rather than a novelist summarizing the chapter's point.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited passages leave no more than two explanatory "the way ..." comparator constructions and no repeated "specific [noun]" tic doing the same job; FAIL if the chapter still turns on the same sentence swivel every few paragraphs.
findings.problem: The prose keeps reaching for the same explanatory scaffolds: repeated "the way ..." comparator sentences and repeated "specific [noun]" labels. After the third instance, the chapter stops feeling rhythmically controlled and starts sounding mechanically generated from its own precision-setting.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:215, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:277, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:281, and trim the repeated "specific ..." phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:311. Keep the observational precision, but let more beats land without the same comparator or qualifier.

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read line 1367 and surrounding context. The phrase 'Tell me something funny' must not appear. The replacement must serve the same scene function (opening the first real working session) in Simone's voice: dry, direct, professional, with no extra words.
findings.problem: Style-bible example line leakage. Simone's calibration-only line 'Tell me something funny' appears verbatim at line 1367. The style bible for simone_barr lists this under example_lines with the 'CALIBRATION ONLY' header.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1367. Replace with a Simone-voice prompt that opens the same scene without echoing the calibration text. Simone's register is dry, direct, economical. Options: she could ask Doug to describe something specific, or give a diagnostic instruction ('Start with yesterday'), or simply wait and let the silence do the prompting — which fits her character's radar for inauthenticity.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's greatest risk is timidity. The premise — a man who is not funny trying to be funny — invites a safe, gently comic execution where Doug is lovably bumbling and everything works out. Resist this. Doug's failures should be genuinely uncomfortable to witness. His loneliness should be genuinely lonely, not charmingly solitary. The toast should contain things that are genuinely inappropriate, not safely edgy. The comedy should come from truth that hurts, not from situations that are merely awkward. The moments of tenderness — the Gloria chapter, the Kelsey lunch, the Simone conversation — should be earned through accumulated specificity, not through the narrative signaling that it's time to feel. Every chapter should contain at least one moment that a cautious writer would soften, and the drafting agent should not soften it.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. If Doug is drunk, describe what drunk looks and feels like — the specific sensory experience, not the label. If Doug is lonely, show the loneliness through the objects and routines that fill the space where other people would be — the cereal dinner, the second bedroom, the unpacked boxes. If a scene involves embarrassment, shame, failure, or longing, render it in full physical and emotional detail. The reader should feel it, not be told about it. Replace every euphemism with the specific thing it is hiding.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Doug going precise under stress, Megan's composed register). If a line reads stiff and the character is not being deliberately formal, contract it.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-4), dialogue density (high-dialogue chapters followed by interior-heavy chapters), pacing (frenetic chapters followed by still ones), emotional register (comic escalation followed by tender quiet), narrative distance (very close interior followed by observed social scenes). The novel should feel like it breathes — tight chapters followed by loose ones, crowded chapters followed by solitary ones. Chapter 3 (quiet office day) must feel structurally different from Chapter 10 (quiet visit to Gloria). Chapter 9 (the corporate disaster) must feel structurally different from Chapter 14 (the tour), even though both involve performance failure. Achieve variety through scene construction, not through gimmick.
prose_style_profile.diction: Mid-register, clean, precise. No pyrotechnics. No extended metaphors. The prose should have the quality of someone describing what happened accurately — not artlessly, but without reaching for beauty. When beauty arrives, it should feel accidental, like Doug's humor: a precisely observed detail that turns out to be devastating not because the prose reached for devastation but because the detail was exact. Insurance language may surface in narration when it captures something emotional more precisely than emotional language would. Avoid lyrical flights, extended similes, or any construction that would sound wrong coming from a narrator who is sympathetic to Doug's plainness.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, tightly bound to Doug's perspective. The narrator has access to Doug's interior — his observations, his assessments, his emotional processing through objects and details — but not to any other character's thoughts. Other characters are rendered through Doug's perception: what they say, how they move, what Doug notices about them. The narrator's voice is distinct from Doug's voice but sympathetic to it — slightly more articulate, slightly more aware, but never mocking and never superior. The humor in the narration comes from the same source as Doug's humor: precision about mundane things that reveals the absurdity or sadness underneath. The narrator does not explain the comedy. The narrator does not wink.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and controlled. Default to mid-length sentences with clean syntax. Use short sentences for comic timing — the flat declarative that follows an elaborate setup. Use longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interior passages where Doug is processing something he cannot name. Avoid uniform sentence length across paragraphs. The prose should breathe — alternating between compression and expansion, between observation and reflection, between the external world and Doug's interior. Comedy requires rhythm more than any other mode: the setup needs room, the punch needs precision, and the space after needs silence.
